title: dodecatheon radicatum subsp. monanthum
intro: Dodecatheon radicatum subsp. monanthum, also known as the alpine shooting star, is a perennial plant species belonging to the gentian family. It is native to subarctic and alpine regions in North America and contains iridoid glycosides, which possess medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, providing potential health benefits such as wound healing, pain relief, and reduced inflammation. Dodecatheon radicatum subsp. monanthum can be prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
